response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be proactive and vigilant. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content, especially if it's spreading quickly. 2) Verify the sources of information before sharing or engaging with them. 3) Use fact-checking tools to debunk false information and promote accurate information. 4) Report suspicious or harmful content to social media platforms. 5) Set your social media privacy settings to limit the visibility of your personal information and content. 6) Use two-factor authentication to protect your accounts from hacking. 7) Be mindful of your online interactions, especially with strangers or unknown sources. By taking these steps, you can significantly reduce the risk of encountering and sharing fake content on social media."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be proactive and vigilant. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content, especially if it's spreading quickly. 2) Verify the sources of information before sharing or engaging with them. 3) Use fact-checking tools to debunk false information and promote accurate information. 4) Report suspicious or harmful content to social media platforms. 5) Set your social media privacy settings to limit the visibility of your personal information and content. 6) Use two-factor authentication to protect your accounts from hacking. 7) Be mindful of your online interactions, especially with strangers or unknown sources. By taking these steps, you can significantly reduce the risk of encountering and sharing fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to be proactive and vigilant. Here are some step-by-step ways to do so: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverifiable content. 2) Verify the sources before sharing or engaging. 3) Use fact-checking tools for debunking false information and promoting accurate ones. 4) Report suspicious or harmful content to social media platforms. 5) Set your privacy settings to limit visibility of personal info & content. 6) Enable two-factor authentication for account security. 7) Be mindful of online interactions, especially with strangers or unknown sources.
